question: Will Karmelo Anthony be convicted of first-degree murder for the killing of Austin Metcalf?
textDescription:
Karmelo Anthony has been charged with first-degree murder. He is accused of stabbing a fellow student, Austin Metcalf, at a high school track meet in Texas.

Will Anthony be convicted of first degree murder, after any appeals have played out?

Conviction for lesser charges won't count. Resolution will depend only on the final legal result of any trials and appeals, i.e. a conviction won't yet count whist there is an unexhausted appeal option.

I lack knowledge about legal processes, but I hope I've made clear that the final outcome being a conviction for first-degree murder is all that matters for this market.

The market will stay open until all proceedings that can affect that outcome have concluded.
